heading: Basic needs allowance for members on active service in the Armed Forces
label: 402b Basic needs allowance for members on active service in the Armed Forces
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, referred to in subsecs. (i) and (j)(1), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 117–81Dec. 27, 2021

children.heading: .—Allowance Required
children.label: a .—Allowance Required
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall pay to each member who is eligible under subsection (b) a basic needs allowance in the amount determined for such member under subsection (c).
the member has completed initial entry training;

the gross household income of the member during the most recent calendar year did not exceed an amount equal to 200 percent of the Federal poverty guidelines of the Department of Health and Human Services for the location of the member and the number of individuals in the household of the member for such year; and

children.label: A
children.text: is not ineligible for the allowance under subsection (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: does not elect under subsection (g) not to receive the allowance.

children.label: A
children.text: 200 percent of the Federal poverty guidelines of the Department of Health and Human Services for the calendar year during which the allowance is paid based on the location of the member and the number of individuals in the household of the member during the month for which the allowance is paid; minus

children.label: B
children.text: the gross household income of the member during the preceding calendar year; divided by

children.label: A
children.text: A member who does not have any dependents.

children.label: B
children.text: A cadet at the United States Military Academy, the United States Air Force Academy, or the Coast Guard Academy, a midshipman at the United States Naval Academy, or a cadet or midshipman serving elsewhere in the armed forces.

children.heading: .—Household with more than one eligible member
children.label: 2 .—Household with more than one eligible member
children.text: In the event a household contains two or more members determined under subsection (f) to be eligible to receive the allowance under subsection (a), only one allowance may be paid to a member among such members as such members shall jointly elect.

children.heading: .—Automatic ineligibility of members receiving certain pay increases
children.label: 3 .—Automatic ineligibility of members receiving certain pay increases
children.text: A member determined to be eligible under subsection (f) for the allowance under subsection (a) whose monthly gross household income increases as a result of a promotion or other permanent increase to pay or allowances under this title to an amount that, on an annualized basis, would exceed the amount described in subsection (b)(2) is ineligible for the allowance. If such member is receiving the allowance, payment of the allowance shall automatically terminate within a reasonable time, as determined by the Secretary of Defense in regulations prescribed under subsection (j).

children.heading: .—Ineligibility of certain changes in income
children.label: 4 .—Ineligibility of certain changes in income
children.text: A member whose gross household income for the preceding year decreases because of a fine, forfeiture, or reduction in rank imposed as a part of disciplinary action or an action under chapter 47 of title 10 (the Uniform Code of Military Justice) is not eligible for the allowance under subsection (a) solely as a result of the fine, forfeiture, or reduction in rank.

children.heading: Application by Members Seeking Allowance.—
children.label: e Application by Members Seeking Allowance.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A member who seeks to receive the allowance under subsection (a) shall submit to the Secretary concerned an application for the allowance that includes such information as the Secretary may require in order to determine whether or not the member is eligible to receive the allowance.

children.heading: .—Timing of submission
children.label: 2 .—Timing of submission
children.text: A member who receives the allowance under subsection (a) and seeks to continue to receive the allowance shall submit to the Secretary concerned an updated application under paragraph (1) at such times as the Secretary may require, but not less frequently than annually.

children.heading: .—Voluntary submission
children.label: 3 .—Voluntary submission
children.text: The submission of an application under paragraph (1) is voluntary.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that all members of the armed forces are screened during initial entry training and regularly thereafter for eligibility for the allowance under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: notify any member so screened who may be eligible that the member may apply for the allowance by submitting an application under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: determine which members of the armed forces are eligible under subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: notify each such member, in writing, of that determination.

children.heading: .—Information included in notice
children.label: 2 .—Information included in notice
children.text: The notice under paragraph (1) shall include information regarding financial management and assistance programs for which the member may be eligible.

children.heading: Election Not to Receive Allowance.—
children.label: g Election Not to Receive Allowance.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A member determined under subsection (f) to be eligible for the allowance under subsection (a) may elect, in writing, not to receive the allowance.

children.heading: .—Deemed ineligible
children.label: 2 .—Deemed ineligible
children.text: A member who does not submit an application under subsection (e) within a reasonable time (as determined by the Secretary concerned) shall be deemed ineligible for the allowance under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Special Rule for Members Stationed Outside United States
children.label: h .—Special Rule for Members Stationed Outside United States
children.text: In the case of a member assigned to a duty location outside the United States, the Secretary concerned shall make the calculations described in subsections (b)(2) and (c)(1) using the Federal poverty guidelines of the Department of Health and Human Services for the continental United States.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: i .—Regulations
children.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, the Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations for the administration of this section.

children.heading: .—Implementation period
children.label: 1 .—Implementation period
children.text: The allowance under subsection (a) is payable for months beginning on or after the date that is one year after the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022.

children.heading: .—Termination
children.label: 2 .—Termination
children.text: The allowance under subsection (a) may not be paid for any month beginning after .December 31, 2027

children.label: A
children.text: all household income, derived from any source; minus

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a member whom the Secretary concerned determines resides in an area with a high cost of living or that otherwise has a demonstrated need, any portion of the basic allowance for housing under that the Secretary concerned elects to exclude.section 403 of this title

children.heading: .—Household
children.label: 2 .—Household
children.text: The term “household” means a member of the armed forces and any dependents of the member enrolled in the Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System, regardless of the location of those dependents.
